# Embodied AI Apprenticeship — Working Rules

## Documentation-as-Teaching Rule

When working on any lab, project, or technical task in this repo:
- Explain the underlying theory in depth as we go (not just "do X")
- Document explanations in `resources/` as reusable theory notes
- Write in a teaching voice suitable for a future book
- Cover: why it works, intuition, common misconceptions, and connections to later courses
- Tag theory docs with which course/lab they relate to

The long-term goal: accumulate enough documented theory and teaching material that it can be compiled into a book on embodied AI from first principles.

## Project Structure

- `curriculum/` — course outlines and lab descriptions
- `projects/` — hands-on implementations (code, configs, diagrams)
- `research_journal/` — engineering reflections, debug logs, decisions
- `resources/` — theory deep-dives, reference material, book-draft content
- `shared/` — cross-project utilities

## Preferences

- No duration estimates (self-directed pace)
- Digital wiring diagrams preferred (Fritzing, KiCad, or Wokwi)
- Explain failures concretely (what you'll observe, not abstract statements)
